reasoning: The baseline rate (as of late July 2025) is 5.85%. Based on the FRED statistical analysis and recent market forecasts (Fannie Mae expects a slight decline over Q3 and Q4 of 2025), the short-term momentum favors a minor decline or stability rather than an increase, keeping the short-term probability below 0.5. Over the medium-to-long term, major institutional forecasters project rates to drift downwards, though uncertainty naturally increases due to variance in macroeconomic conditions and the exact trajectory of Fed rate cuts. As mandated by best practices for forecasting over longer horizons, my estimates pull closer to 50% (maximum uncertainty) for dates further in the future.
